key_highlights: U.S. added 172,000 jobs in May, unemployment steady at 4.3%, fueling Fed rate-hike bets and a sharp tech sell-off.
Nasdaq 100 plunged 4.8-5% in an AI-led rout, while the Dow surged 500-800 points on rotation into defensive and value sectors.
SpaceX IPO at $1.77 trillion valuation dominates headlines, but index inclusion may be delayed, with indirect ownership via Alphabet considered.
Gold erased 2026 gains as strong jobs data boosted dollar and bond yields, while oil fell on demand destruction fears.
Geopolitical risks persist: US-Iran conflict nears 100 days, and a flesh-eating screwworm threatens U.S. cattle.
Corporate earnings and AI-driven CapEx remain key to sustained gains, with Broadcom earnings triggering tech volatility.
Private credit deterioration remains slow and contained, per analysts, while Goldman Sachs views recent share sales as a healthy market signal.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off, Nasdaq 100 down ~5%, led by AI and chip stocks after Broadcom earnings and strong jobs data raised rate-hike fears. Rotation out of growth into value.
sectors.implications: Near-term volatility likely; AI CapEx remains a key driver but higher rates compress valuations. Opportunities in cybersecurity (Crowdstrike, Palo Alto) and data center infrastructure (Eaton, nVent).

sectors.trend: Strong outperformance; Dow jumped 500-800 points on rotation into banks and retail. JPMorgan may buy DFA; Goldman and Morgan Stanley decked out in SpaceX gear.
sectors.implications: Higher rates benefit net interest margins. M&A activity (JPM/DFA) and IPO underwriting fees (SpaceX) provide catalysts. Buy signals on health insurers (UNH, CVS).

sectors.trend: Oil falls on demand destruction and stalled talks; gold erases 2026 gains as dollar strengthens. Copper and silver stocks see optimism from EV transition and infrastructure spending.
sectors.implications: Energy infrastructure and electrical contracting stocks (EME, ETN, APG) benefit from data center buildout. Precious metals face headwinds from strong dollar and rate hikes.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Lululemon cut outlook on undisclosed headwinds; Uber slashed people team by 23%. Ulta poised for rebound. Knicks NBA Finals boost MSG investors.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending under pressure from inflation and higher rates. Selective opportunities in beauty (Ulta) and entertainment (MSG).

sectors.trend: Health insurers (UNH, CVS) flashing buy signals. Eli Lilly sees more dealmaking leveraging GLP-1 success. AI disruption fears hit Solventum.
sectors.implications: GLP-1 and AI in healthcare remain key themes. Managed care names offer defensive value. M&A pipeline active.

sectors.trend: Defense budget proposal boosts Kratos (KTOS); Pelican Products makes big bet on defense. SpaceX IPO highlights space economy.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical tensions and increased defense spending support long-term growth. Space and defense contractors are key beneficiaries.

key_themes: Strong labor market fuels Fed rate-hike expectations, causing a massive rotation out of tech into value/defensive.
SpaceX IPO at $1.77T valuation is a major event, but index inclusion may be delayed; indirect ownership via Alphabet considered.
AI and data center CapEx remain critical for growth, but higher rates pressure valuations; electrical contracting stocks benefit.
Geopolitical risks: US-Iran conflict, World Cup security logistics, and screwworm threat to cattle.
Private credit deterioration slow and contained; Goldman views share sales as healthy.
Consumer sector under pressure: Lululemon cuts outlook, Uber cuts staff, but Ulta and Knicks show pockets of strength.
Gold and silver miners face headwinds from strong dollar; copper and EV transition metals see long-term optimism.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Rotate into value/defensive sectors (financials, healthcare, energy infrastructure). Reduce duration in bond portfolios. Consider floating-rate instruments.
Trim overvalued AI names; increase exposure to cybersecurity (Crowdstrike, Palo Alto) and data center infrastructure (Eaton, nVent) with strong fundamentals. Use options for hedging.
Increase exposure to defense (KTOS, Pelican) and energy infrastructure. Maintain cash reserves for opportunistic buying. Monitor oil supply disruptions.
Avoid discretionary names with weak guidance (Lululemon, Uber). Favor discount retailers and essential services. Consider Ulta for a rebound play.
Diversify across metals: copper/EV transition (RIO, FCX, BHP) for long-term, but reduce gold exposure on strong dollar. Use futures/options for hedging.
Monitor but no immediate action; current data shows slow, contained deterioration. Main Street Capital (MAIN) remains a solid BDC pick for income.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Eaton (ETN) and nVent Electric (NVT) on weakness for data center infrastructure exposure.
opportunities.rationale: AI and data center buildout drive multi-year demand for electrical equipment. Analysts raising expectations; strong secular tailwinds.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Crowdstrike (CRWD) and Palo Alto Networks (PANW) for cybersecurity growth.
opportunities.rationale: AI disruption fears boost demand for cybersecurity. Both have strong fundamentals and high switching costs. Recent sell-off offers entry.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider indirect SpaceX exposure via Alphabet (GOOGL) or buy defense/space ETFs.
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX IPO at $1.77T may face index inclusion delays. Alphabet's stake provides indirect exposure with less volatility. Defense budget boosts Kratos (KTOS).

opportunities.recommendation: Buy UnitedHealth (UNH) and CVS Health (CVS) for defensive healthcare exposure.
opportunities.rationale: Health insurers flashing buy signals; managed care offers stability amid rate uncertainty. GLP-1 tailwinds benefit UNH.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in copper and EV transition metals via Rio Tinto (RIO) and Freeport-McMoRan (FCX).
opportunities.rationale: Long-term demand from electrification and infrastructure. Analysts raising targets; operational improvements expected.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce tech/growth exposure and rotate into financials (JPM, GS) and value sectors.
defensive_moves.rationale: Strong jobs data and potential rate hike favor banks and value. JPMorgan M&A and SpaceX underwriting provide catalysts.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put options on QQQ or buy inverse tech ETFs.
defensive_moves.rationale: Nasdaq 100 correction may continue as rate fears persist. Protect against further downside in AI and growth stocks.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation and short-duration bonds.
defensive_moves.rationale: Bond yields rising on rate-hike bets; cash provides optionality to buy dips. Short-duration TIPS protect against inflation.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Sell Lululemon (LULU) and Uber (UBER) on any bounces.
defensive_moves.rationale: Weak guidance and cost-cutting signal consumer headwinds. Avoid until clarity on demand improves.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Maintain gold exposure only as a small hedge; prefer copper miners for growth.
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold erased 2026 gains due to strong dollar and rate hikes. Copper benefits from structural demand.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Continued volatility driven by Fed rate path, tech rotation, and geopolitical tensions. The strong May jobs report (172K) reduces chances of rate cuts and may lead to a hike. Expect further rotation out of AI/tech into value, financials, and defensive sectors. Gold and bonds under pressure; dollar strength persists. Key events: May CPI (June 12), Fed meeting (June 17-18), SpaceX IPO pricing.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Positive for infrastructure, cybersecurity, and energy transition. AI CapEx remains a multi-year driver, but valuations need to reset. SpaceX IPO could catalyze space economy. Geopolitical risks (US-Iran, World Cup) may create buying opportunities. Consumer sector recovery hinges on inflation easing. Private credit remains stable. Long-term winners: data center infrastructure, cybersecurity, copper, and managed care.
key_catalysts: May CPI inflation report (June 12) – will influence Fed decision.
Fed meeting (June 17-18) – rate hike or hawkish dot plot could deepen tech sell-off.
SpaceX IPO pricing and debut – market reception and index inclusion decisions.
Broadcom and other AI earnings – set tone for AI trade.
US-Iran conflict developments – impact on oil and defense.
World Cup security logistics – boost for security and logistics firms.
Lululemon and Uber earnings – consumer health barometer.
watch_list: 10-year Treasury yield (currently rising on rate-hike bets).
Dollar index (DXY) – strength pressuring EM and commodities.
Nasdaq 100 vs. Dow ratio – rotation indicator.
Gold price – below 2026 levels; watch for support.
Oil price – demand destruction vs. geopolitical risk.
Bitcoin – crashed below $64K; risk appetite gauge.
VIX – volatility index likely to stay elevated.
SpaceX IPO roadshow and institutional demand.
Private credit default rates – currently contained.
